ip_outcome_note: Vision pass explicitly identified black-and-gold Saiyan battle armor, widow's-peak upward hair, and stern authoritative posture — all signature visual identifiers of Vegeta. Card name on the card face is 'Vegeta', special trait is 'Saiyan', and the card is flagged Resurrection F Saga era. Identity is confirmed by card name + canonical visual identifiers; no ambiguity.

trivia_bullets.text: Galactic Battle (BT1) was the inaugural main booster set for the Dragon Ball Super Card Game, released July 28, 2017. It contained 114 cards (60 Common, 30 Uncommon, 12 Rare, 8 Super Rare, 4 Special Rare), making BT1-038 part of the founding print run of the entire game. [PSA CardFacts / The Games Corner AU]
sources: PSA CardFacts: 2017 Dragon Ball Super Series 1 Galactic Battle

trivia_bullets.text: BT1-038 is assigned to the Resurrection F Saga era — the story arc in which Frieza is revived and immediately resumes his vendetta against Vegeta and Goku. In the arc, Vegeta trains off-planet with the angel Whis, and during the final battle is killed when Frieza destroys Earth — an event reversed by Whis rewinding time. [Wikipedia: Vegeta]
sources: WebSearch snippet via Dragon Ball Wiki

trivia_bullets.text: BT1-038 is a Blue Battle card with 20,000 Power and 5,000 Combo Power, costing 3 Blue energy. Its adjacent common-run neighbor BT1-037 'Assailant Vegeta' (Uncommon, 15,000 Power) carries the [Critical] keyword — when it inflicts damage, the opponent places those cards in the Drop Area instead of their hand. The two cards form a small Vegeta common-tier block within BT1's Blue color. [DBZ Exchange / CoreTCG / ToyWiz via WebSearch snippets]

At least five distinct Vegeta cards appear in the BT1 set at different rarities: BT1-028 (Rare Leader / Super Saiyan Blue Vegeta flip), BT1-036 (God Charge Vegeta), BT1-037 (Assailant Vegeta, Uncommon), BT1-038 (this card, Common), and BT1-064 (Raging Attacker Vegeta, Rare). The density of Vegeta representations across every rarity tier in the inaugural set reflects his co-protagonist billing alongside Goku in Dragon Ball Super's early marketing. [WebSearch snippets: Bleedingcool checklist / retailer listings]

Vegeta is the prince of the Saiyan race and son of King Vegeta; the planet bearing their name was destroyed by Frieza, who feared the Saiyan race's potential to produce a Super Saiyan. Vegeta survived because he ignored Frieza's recall order. His title 'Prince of all Saiyans' is the core of his identity throughout Dragon Ball Super — a dynasty without a planet, a king without a kingdom. [Wikipedia: Vegeta / Dragon Ball Wiki via WebSearch]
sources: WebSearch snippet: CBR — Vegeta Saiyan royalty retcon
